message:
test(fcp-core): pin string-id size-class Eq+Hash semantics (v112c)

Add crates/fcp-core/tests/string_id_size_class_eq_hash.rs pinning
the Hash-then-Equality contract on canonical fcp-core IDs across
content size classes.

Bead asks for "CompactString-backed identifier" coverage; no
CompactString (compact_str crate) is in use in fcp-core or anywhere
else in the workspace at the time of writing — the canonical IDs are
Arc<str> backed (CapabilityId, InstanceId, ZoneId, PrincipalId) or
String backed (quorum::NodeId). The observable contract is identical
either way: equal values MUST hash identically regardless of
construction path OR content size class.

Companion test hash_eq_contract.rs (llp14) covered the construction-
path axis; this test adds the orthogonal content-size axis. 11 tests
sweep:

- 1-byte (smallest legal canonical id)
- 23-byte (last inline size if compact_str is ever adopted)
- 24-byte (first heap size for compact_str)
- 50, 100, 128-byte (well past any inline boundary; 128 is the
  documented canonical-id length cap)

Per size class: each ID type is constructed via every available path
(new / try_from / FromStr / clone / Display roundtrip / String vs
&str), and the Hash-Eq contract is verified via DefaultHasher.
Cross-size HashMap-key correctness pinned: short and long IDs serve
as distinct keys and look up correctly via re-constructed values.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
